summary: First pass toward building with CodeWarrior's `-requireprotos` option. The PR mostly added missing function prototypes to headers across Dolphin AI/AR, sysdolphin baselib, and several fighter character modules, especially Master Hand, CLink, Crazy Hand, and Game & Watch. It also adjusted a few function declarations/definitions to use explicit `void` parameter lists, exposed `__ARHandler` by removing `static`, added per-C-file headers for newly prototyped functions, and normalized some `struct _HSD_GObj*` prototypes to `HSD_GObj*` after including the proper gobj header.
what_changed: Added missing Dolphin AI prototypes for `AIResetStreamSampleCount` and `AISetStreamTrigger` in `src/dolphin/ai/ai.h`.

Completed `src/dolphin/ar/ar.h` with prototypes for AR helpers and API functions including `__ARChecksize`, `ARGetBaseAddress`, `ARGetSize`, `ARGetDMAStatus`, `ARAlloc`, `ARFree`, `ARCheckInit`, and `ARInit`.
Changed `ARCheckInit()` to `ARCheckInit(void)` in both declaration and definition, making the no-argument prototype explicit for `-requireprotos`.

Removed `static` from `__ARHandler` in `ar.c`, likely so its definition is compatible with a required external prototype or cross-file reference; the diff does not show a new header declaration for `__ARHandler`.
Collapsed multi-line function declarations for inline/asm handlers in OS files, including `DecrementerExceptionHandler` and `ExternalInterruptHandler`, without changing apparent behavior.
Added a large set of missing fighter callback/action prototypes to `ftclink.h`, including CLink load, death, item, knockback, and special-attribute functions.
Added Crazy Hand prototypes for `ftCrazyhand_OnDeath`, `ftCrazyhand_OnLoad`, and `ftCrazyHand_LoadSpecialAttrs`.

Created `ftGameWatch_SpecialN.h` for the float-order helper functions `ftGameWatch_SpecialN_SetFloatOrder1/2/3` and included it from `ftGameWatch_SpecialN.c` before the broader `ftgamewatch.h` include.
Added or expanded many Master Hand numbered headers (`ftMasterHand_10.h` through `ftMasterHand_20.h`) with prototypes for `lbl_...` action-state helpers and existing `func_...` exports.
Updated several Master Hand C files to include their matching numbered header, such as `ftMasterHand_12.c`, `ftMasterHand_13.c`, and `ftMasterHand_19.c`.
Replaced some prototypes using `struct _HSD_GObj*` with `HSD_GObj*` after adding or relying on `<sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h>` includes.
smart_moves: Used small, local headers for split character source files instead of dumping every symbol into a monolithic character header; examples include `ftGameWatch_SpecialN.h`, `ftclink_2.h`, and new numbered Master Hand headers.
Made no-argument functions explicit with `(void)`, as shown by `ARCheckInit(void)`, which is required by strict prototype checking and avoids old-style C ambiguity.

Included the defining header from the corresponding `.c` file so the compiler can check that the function definition matches its declaration, e.g. `ftGameWatch_SpecialN.c` includes `ftGameWatch_SpecialN.h` and Master Hand numbered `.c` files include their numbered headers.
Added `<sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h>` where headers use `HSD_GObj*`, allowing cleaner typedef-based prototypes instead of incomplete `struct _HSD_GObj*` spellings.
Kept unresolved decomp names (`func_...`, `lbl_...`) rather than inventing semantic names during a mechanical prototype-enablement pass.
decomp_lessons: When enabling `-requireprotos`, every non-static function definition needs a visible prototype before the definition; add a matching local/module header and include it at the top of the implementation file.
For functions with no parameters, use `void` in both declaration and definition. `foo()` is not a strict prototype in C and can fail under stricter compile settings.
Header cleanup can expose linkage mismatches: if a function must be externally visible or prototyped outside its translation unit, its definition cannot remain `static`.
Prefer including the type's real header, such as `gobj.h`, over relying on repeated `struct _HSD_GObj*` forward declarations when standardizing prototypes.
Mechanical prototype work should preserve matching-sensitive function bodies and naming. This PR mainly touches headers and declarations, with minimal implementation changes.

Split-file fighter modules benefit from per-file headers named after the source shard (`ftMasterHand_12.h`, `ftGameWatch_SpecialN.h`) to satisfy strict prototype checking without over-centralizing declarations.
naming_conventions: New header guards follow path-derived uppercase names, e.g. `MELEE_FT_CHARA_FTCLINK_FTCLINK_2_H`, `MELEE_FT_CHARA_FTGAMEWATCH_FTGAMEWATCH_SPECIALN_H`, and `MELEE_FT_CHARA_FTMASTERHAND_FTMASTERHAND_20_H`.
Unidentified functions retain address-based names such as `func_80149354` and label-based names such as `lbl_80152174` until stronger semantic evidence exists.

Fighter lifecycle names use the existing pattern `ft<Character>_OnLoad`, `ft<Character>_OnDeath`, `ft<Character>_LoadSpecialAttrs`, `ft<Character>_OnItemPickup`, and `ft<Character>_OnKnockbackEnter/Exit`.
CLink prototypes use argument names like `gobj` and typed `HSD_GObj*`, while some Master Hand prototypes still use generic `arg0` where meaning is unknown.
The PR shows inconsistent capitalization already present in names such as `ftCrazyhand_OnDeath` versus `ftCrazyHand_LoadSpecialAttrs`; no evidence suggests the PR attempted to rename these for style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The OSAlarm inline assembly function declaration for `DecrementerExceptionHandler` was reformatted onto one line while preserving the `asm void` function body and `nofralloc` block.
The OSInterrupt forward declaration for `ExternalInterruptHandler` was similarly kept as a `static` prototype but reformatted onto one line.
The changes are matching-safe in spirit: mostly declarations, includes, and whitespace around prototypes/asm wrappers rather than control-flow or codegen-sensitive body edits.
Adding each implementation's own header before other includes is a compile-check tactic for prototype correctness under `-requireprotos`.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the supplied PR slice.
One review is recorded in the metadata, but the excerpt contains no review body or actionable feedback, so reviewer intent cannot be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs titled or mentioning `requireprotos` to see subsequent passes and whether remaining translation units were made strict-prototype clean.
Search for `__ARHandler` declarations after PR 695 to determine where its non-static linkage was intended to be consumed.
Search for headers still using `struct _HSD_GObj*` to continue typedef/header normalization.
Audit newly added `lbl_...` Master Hand prototypes for later semantic renames once action-state behavior is understood.
Check whether all `.c` files with external function definitions include their own matching header before broader project headers.
